title: Understanding Angular Velocity
text: Angular velocity is the rate of change of angular displacement and is usually measured in radians per second.

title: Relating Linear and Angular Quantities
text: The relationship between linear distance traveled along a circle and angular displacement is given by the formula: \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ \frac{s}{r} \), where \( s \) is the arc length and \( $$r$$ \) is the radius.

title: Calculating Angular Displacement
text: Given that the particle moves $$3.0$$ $$m$$ along a circle of radius $$1.5$$ $$m$$, calculate the angular displacement \( $$\theta$$ \) using the formula \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ \frac{s}{r} \).

title: Calculate Angular Displacement
text: Use the formula \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ \frac{s}{r} \) to find the angular displacement. \( s $$=$$ $$3.0$$ \) $$m$$ and \( $$r$$ $$=$$ $$1.5$$ \) $$m$$.

title: Finding Angular Velocity
text: Angular velocity \( \omega \) is calculated using the formula \( \omega $$=$$ \frac{\theta}{t} \), where \( $$\theta$$ \) is the angular displacement and \( $$t$$ \) is the time taken.

title: Calculate Angular Velocity
text: Use the formula \( \omega $$=$$ \frac{\theta}{t} \) to find the angular velocity. \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ $$2$$ \) radians and \( $$t$$ $$=$$ $$1.0$$ \) s.
